reviews.author: Rico Trebeljahr
reviews.text: There's a long and a short tour when we went for 45.000 and 195.000 each and I can't stress the difference between the two enough. All the amazingly beautiful and breathtaking excursion is locked behind the long tour and it is worth *every* penny. Our guide was also such a lovely human, waiting for us taking so so many photos and just being really kind and knowledgeable, it was a beautiful adventure and the pictures, even though stunning, don't even do this place justice. It's *massive* and visually overwhelming, and the lights are done very tastefully, just such a wonderful and crazy experience overall.

reviews.author: yanerps
reviews.text: We took the long guided tour on a weekday. There wasn't any crowd at all going in, but saw a few couples on the way back. The long tour was very interesting! The short route doesn't give the cave justice because there's so much more to explore. Our guide, Ian (not sure how to spell her name) was very sweet, lively and attentive. She gave her best effort despite the language barrier. Really appreciate it. Surprisingly there's even internet coverage deeeep inside the cave 😆 Would definitely recommend the long route if you have the time. Difficulty: 3/5 Time taken: 2 hours+ Distance covered: 3 km FEES 💸 ➡️ Motorcycle parking: 10,000 VND - in front of the coffee vendor. Their coffee was super good btw! ➡️ Entrance fee: 45,000 VND / pax ➡️ Long guided tour: 150,000 VND / pax

reviews.author: Philip Blair
reviews.text: Very large and extensive cave system. Fairly easy to walk as all designated paths that we took were level and solid. Be aware that there are a few places where you need to duck down to pass. The lighting is underwhelming which is a shame since the various caverns are impressive. We visited in November 2025 and we seemed to have the place to ourselves. There is an option to make a much longer visit than we did.

reviews.author: Minty Vo
reviews.text: Nguom Ngao Cave is truly beautiful. After visiting Sung Sot Cave in Ha Long, I actually like this cave even more. I arrived right at 7:50 AM, and it was very quiet. I had plenty of time to walk, look around, and enjoy the cave without the crowds like in Sung Sot. The rock formations and the atmosphere inside are stunning. Definitely worth a visit if you’re in Cao Bang.

reviews.author: TravelbyTin
reviews.text: Wow, 6hour explore the dark cave with the local people, lovely adventure and awesome stuffs to see. High recommended

reviews.author: Nuve SV
reviews.text: A nice, easy to walk, path well maintained, but does not have the wow factor for me. Worth a visit if you in the area, but not worth driving all the way just to see the cave.

reviews.author: Вікторія Якобше
reviews.text: This cave is incredible! It’s huge and very well maintained. I highly recommend taking the longer route with a guide, because the most beautiful parts are there. P.S. Make sure to ask for an English-speaking guide — mine spoke only Vietnamese, which was still fun!

reviews.author: Rosie Cole
reviews.text: We went at 8am and only a few other people there, recommend to go early if you can. We thought it was great, lots of v I pressure stalactites and flow formations (and I've been to Phong nha caves) we even spotted quite a large snake inside. There are a couple of low sections you have to creep under so be aware if you might struggle with that - see video

reviews.author: Mariana
reviews.text: Went on Saturday in January and we were practically the only people inside. Quite beautiful and well lit. The short route is about 1 km - it has a different exit, you don’t have to return. The entrance ticket is 45 k per person

reviews.author: John Winter
reviews.text: Nguom Ngao Caves blew me away. Easily one of the best cave systems I’ve ever explored — the natural formations are massive and stunning, with layers of stalactites and stalagmites lit beautifully to showcase the full scale and texture. The internal layout is superb, with safe, well-paved paths and solid railings throughout. It’s also surprisingly modern, with multiple 4G/5G antennas discreetly integrated into the cave network, which helps with safety and connectivity. Highly recommend this to anyone visiting northern Vietnam — a must-see if you enjoy natural wonders! Thursday 7:30 AM–5 PM Friday 7:30 AM–5 PM Saturday 7:30 AM–5 PM Sunday 7:30 AM–5 PM Monday 7:30 AM–5 PM Tuesday 7:30 AM–5 PM Wednesday 7:30 AM–5 PM

reviews.author: Dream
reviews.text: I'm not a massive cave visiting tourist but this cave is pretty cool I enjoyed my visit and for only 45k it's a bargain. Also the drive to here is beautiful and highly recommended on scooter. Enjoy.

reviews.author: Han Nguyen
reviews.text: The cave is over 400 million years and first discovered in 1921. Amazing cave. One of must-visit attractions in Cao Bang. Special note: 02:00 PM on April 22 yearly, three beams of sunlight enter the cave through a natural opening in the ceiling. This rare optical phenomenon lasts only a few minutes and happens just once a year.

reviews.author: Belén Castelló
reviews.text: I was staying nearby and thought of checking the caves early in the morning. It was such a pleasant surprise as I didn’t have my expectations too high. I arrived early, around 8:15 and had the place to myself. Big bus tours arrived around 8:45. Totally recommend you visit early if you want a special experience! Entrance was 45VND.

reviews.author: Tom
reviews.text: Amazing cave! Great guide! Pay the 195k so you can see all 2km of this huge cave. There is a long legged centipede in there, he is palm sized. Tell him I say hello :)

reviews.author: Grace Kelly Hosea
reviews.text: You should pay a visit to this cave. Explore the amazing cave of the tigers in the past (?) There are lots to see inside: The lotus stone with water streams, crystal tree, amazing stones in shapes. Lighting inside is good, safe. Wear proper shoes, a bit slippery. You might go alone or with friends, no need for guides (my suggestion) The ticket box staff were a bit of ignorance.

reviews.author: Matthew
reviews.text: Loved it, for any cave nerd or anyone in the area absolutely come and have a walk, price is cheap and the caves were really cool, you have 2 options for a short or long walk, we did short and it took about 15-20 minutes but it had a cool scale and loved it.

reviews.author: Steve Mitchell
reviews.text: Went there today, paid for the longer route and soooo worth it. We have visited Hang En in Phong Nhã and this cave was as impressive. Some of the pictures look great, but it will never do it justice. AWESOME!

reviews.author: So M
reviews.text: A cave that used to be full of tigers, now an attraction with various limestones and rock formations. It is quite clean and not as commercialised as other limestone caves in tourist areas. The cave is quite big and much cooler inside. Not many people either, so worth a visit, but make sure you visit the bathroom before you go inside (it's a bit of a walk from the entry to the end). 👍😉

reviews.author: Betty Li
reviews.text: It was okay. Some of the stairs were super steep, some were slippery, and there were even 2 places where you had to bend right down to pass through. Not what I would consider easy for older or physically challenged people, like me.

reviews.author: Tammy Pyne
reviews.text: An absolutely amazing cave, with fantastic formations and glittering stalagmites. Be aware that you have to bend down a bit to get through some sections and the floor can be very slippery and wet but definitely worth visiting.

reviews.author: Chuyền Tony
reviews.text: Nguom Ngao or Tiger’s Cave it once of many other beautiful caves in Cao Bang. To came here it was very easy now after you pack your motorbikes at motorbikes packing . You have to buy tickets the staff’s gonna so you which way into for costumes scan tickets. He also recommended that you could join another plane to explore more inside the cave the new part( if you have time you can join this trip full day inside the cave) Inside of the cave have many stalactites and stalagmites it over than thousands years ago , there also have light and the sidewalks . The cave it was bigger here . Could be five thousand square miles. You could spend 30 minutes to 1 hours for walk through the cave 3.5 miles from Ban Gioc Waterfall

reviews.author: Leona Joyfull Life
reviews.text: Nguom Ngao Cave is one of the largest caves in the North. About 3 km from Ban Gioc Waterfall. Inside the cave is large, with many stalactites. There are 2 tours to visit the cave. The tour is about 1 km away, the tour time is about 1 hour, a ticket price is 45,000 VND/pax. The experience tour is about 2-3 hours, including a tour and deep exploration with quite rugged terrain, a personal tour guide will support you, ticket price is 195,000 VND/pax. I think you should come early because the cave is quite dark, especially the experience tour. You also need a good pair of shoes to ensure safety when the cave floor is slippery. Because the cave has a stream. The cave is very beautiful. In addition, if you love exploring caves. You should think of Phong Nha Ke Bang in Quang Binh province. There are Phong Nha cave, Thien Duong cave, Son Doong cave... and thousands of caves. I have a few videos there. #handinhandadventure

reviews.author: Mia M
reviews.text: Beautiful cave the only reason I’m giving it 4 stars is because I slipped a bunch and thought I was going to die lol

reviews.author: nex
reviews.text: absolutely stunning cave. very extensive and lots of different structures and formations to see. wasn't too busy which was nice, lighting wasn't the clearest but it made the ambiance better, plus the light is bad for cave ecosystems anyway so i thought it was fine. it was unfortunate, halfway through my walk the power went out and we just had to use phone flashlights to get out. didn't get to explore the whole cave because of that.

reviews.author: Vic L
reviews.text: One of the best caves in Vietnam. Don’t understand why it is not yet popular in attraction list of the world. Highly recommended.

reviews.author: Mimi Menze
reviews.text: Very nice and big cave, but be careful when you are tall, there are 2 places where it's very smal. Interesting that you can make 2 different tours, normal tour by yourself and a guided tour for about 2km

reviews.author: Gregg Valentine
reviews.text: Pretty cool cave that goes under three mountains. The tour and parking ran me 200K vnd, which is about $8 US. They provided water shoes, a locker, a reflective vest, and a headlamp. The tour traversed what felt like a few kilometers of cave with many types of formations. Our guide spoke a little bit of English but mostly Vietnamese. I might suggest reading up about limestone caves and some of what goes on to contextualize for yourself before taking the tour. Also, people tried to charge me to park in the lot closest to the cave entrance. Don't bother with all that. It cost 5K vnd to park in the lot near the ticket booth, it's very close to the cave entrance.

reviews.author: Nickk
reviews.text: Absolutely stunning has the whole place to myself. Take ya time as you'd probably never see something as stunning as before. I thought the lights would be a problem but they helped with the photos suprisingly. It's low season so I had the whole cave to myself

reviews.author: Thu Nga Nguyen
reviews.text: It's spectacular, well worth the visit during our motobike trip in Northern Vietnam

reviews.author: sophie fewkes
reviews.text: I wasn’t going to bother coming here but it was rainy and I wanted to be inside and honestly I’m so glad I went! 45k entrance and 10k for undercover bike parking and I spent half an hour wandering the cave, which I had all to myself. I could’ve walked further but chose the shorter path. There was good lighting and pathways, which I appreciated. Very easy to find.
